chief_complaint: A 50-year-old woman presented with a left-sided weakness.
history: The patient presented with a left lacunar infarct. She was incidentally found to have congenital absence of the ipsilateral internal carotid artery (ICA). No conventional vascular risk factors were identified.
exam: Vital signs were within normal limits. Physical examination revealed a left-sided weakness.
labs: Cerebral perfusion was preserved. Imaging confirmed the presence of a hypoplastic bony carotid canal, consistent with congenital origin.
correct_diagnosis: Lacunar stroke with incidental congenital absence of the internal carotid artery
differentials: Lacunar stroke with incidental congenital absence of the internal carotid artery
correct_ranking: Lacunar stroke with incidental congenital absence of the internal carotid artery
